title: Closed-Loop Stepper Motor with AS5600 Magnetic Encoder
design_question: How to detect and correct missed steps on a stepper motor without switching to a full servo drive system?
selected_approach: NEMA17 stepper motor (1.8°/step) + A4988 driver + AS5600 absolute magnetic encoder (I²C 0x36, 12-bit, 0.088°/LSB) mounted to shaft rear. Neodymium diametrically magnetised disc magnet (6 × 2.5 mm) on shaft. MCU interrupt-driven step scheduler at 1 kHz; encoder polled at same rate; error > 5 counts triggers catch-up steps; error > 40 counts (3.5°) declares stall.
rationale: Standard open-loop steppers skip steps under overload without any feedback. AS5600 adds absolute position tracking with 0.088° resolution — finer than 1/16 microstepping. The feedback loop costs only £2 (AS5600 chip) and a magnet. Eliminates home-cycle requirements if AS5600 is set to ZERO on startup.

alternatives.approach: Full servo motor + driver (e.g., NEMA17 servo with encoder)
alternatives.reason_rejected: Servo drives are 10–30× more expensive for comparable torque; closed-loop stepper sufficient for low-speed, moderate-accuracy applications like CNC pen plotters.
Sensorless stall detection works only at medium–high speeds; AS5600 encoder provides reliable feedback at zero speed and during slow moves.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Stepper Motor Closed-Loop Control via Magnetic Encoder Feedback' confirms AS5600 0.088°/LSB resolution and describes stall-threshold correction loop.
supporting_fact_titles: Stepper Motor Closed-Loop Control via Magnetic Encoder Feedback
Stepper Motor Drive — Full Step and Microstepping
